description: The debate over the FDA's 1998 approval of Celgene's Thalomid (thalidomide) for leprosy, following its history as a cause of birth defects. The controversy centered on whether strict distribution controls could prevent a repeat of the 1950s tragedy.

key_events.description: Celgene proposes a mandatory registry system for doctors and patients.
FDA advisory committee votes 11-0 in favor of approval with strict risk management.
Public hearings feature emotional testimony from original thalidomide victims.
FDA officially approves the drug with the 'STEPS' monitoring program.

calibration_notes: Tests the balance between historical trauma/stigma and scientific data-driven risk management.
